core_mechanism: Lanczos/Krylov tridiagonalization: iterated application of a linear evolution operator to a seed vector, orthogonalized under a chosen inner product, reducing the operator's action to a three-term recurrence (a one-dimensional chain with recursion coefficients)
brief_reason: The abstract builds a Lanczos recursion in which Poisson brackets act as the generating operator and phase-space integrals supply the inner product, producing a Krylov chain whose depth quantifies complexity.
